en.q: According to the law of reflection, the angle of incidence and the angle of reflection are ____.
en.exp: Summary: i = r for all reflection.
en.hint: Summary: i = r for all reflection.

zhHant.q: 根據反射定律，入射角與反射角的關係是____。
zhHant.exp: 總結：入射角 = 反射角。
zhHant.hint: 總結：入射角 = 反射角。

en.q: The angle of incidence is measured between the incident ray and the ____.
en.exp: Angles are measured from the normal, not the surface.
en.hint: Angles are measured from the normal, not the surface.

zhHant.q: 入射角是入射光線與哪一條線的夾角？
zhHant.exp: 角度應與法線量度，不是與鏡面。
zhHant.hint: 角度應與法線量度，不是與鏡面。

en.q: Regular (specular) reflection occurs mainly on a ____ surface.
en.exp: Smooth polished surfaces give parallel reflected rays and clear images.
en.hint: Smooth polished surfaces give parallel reflected rays and clear images.

zhHant.q: 規則反射主要發生在____表面。
zhHant.exp: 光滑表面反射光線互相平行，可形成清晰像。
zhHant.hint: 光滑表面反射光線互相平行，可形成清晰像。

en.q: Diffuse reflection occurs on a ____ surface and does not form a clear image.
en.exp: Rough surfaces scatter reflected rays in many directions.
en.hint: Rough surfaces scatter reflected rays in many directions.

zhHant.q: 漫反射發生在____表面，不能形成清晰像。
zhHant.exp: 粗糙表面使反射光向各方向散射。
zhHant.hint: 粗糙表面使反射光向各方向散射。

en.q: The image formed by a plane mirror is always ____.
en.exp: Plane mirror images are virtual; rays only appear to meet behind the mirror.
en.hint: Plane mirror images are virtual.

zhHant.q: 平面鏡所成的像必定是____。
zhHant.exp: 平面鏡成虛像，光線延長線在鏡後相交。
zhHant.hint: 平面鏡成虛像，光線延長線在鏡後相交。

en.q: A plane mirror image is ____ the object and is laterally inverted.
en.exp: Image height equals object height; left and right appear swapped.
en.hint: Image height equals object height.

zhHant.q: 平面鏡像與物體比較，大小____，且左右倒轉。
zhHant.exp: 像高與物高相同，並有左右倒轉。
zhHant.hint: 像高與物高相同，並有左右倒轉。

en.q: For a plane mirror, the object distance u and the image distance v satisfy ____.
en.exp: The image is as far behind the mirror as the object is in front.
en.hint: The image is as far behind the mirror as the object is in front.

zhHant.q: 平面鏡的物距 u 與像距 v 滿足____。
zhHant.exp: 像距等於物距（虛像在鏡後）。
zhHant.hint: 像距等於物距（虛像在鏡後）。

en.q: The minimum length of a plane mirror for a person to see their full height is about ____ of their height.
en.exp: Summary: minimum mirror length ≈ ½ × height (eyes to feet).
en.hint: Summary: minimum mirror length ≈ ½ × height (eyes to feet).

zhHant.q: 要看見全身，平面鏡的最小長度約為身高的____。
zhHant.exp: 總結：最小鏡長約為身高的一半。
zhHant.hint: 總結：最小鏡長約為身高的一半。

en.q: Light from a very distant object is usually treated as ____ rays.
en.exp: Far-away rays arriving at the eye are nearly parallel.
en.hint: Far-away rays arriving at the eye are nearly parallel.

zhHant.q: 很遠的物體發出的光線通常可視為____。
zhHant.exp: 遠距離光線接近平行。
zhHant.hint: 遠距離光線接近平行。

en.q: If a plane mirror rotates through angle θ (incident ray fixed), the reflected ray turns through ____.
en.exp: Mirror rotation θ changes the reflected direction by 2θ.
en.hint: Mirror rotation θ changes the reflected direction by 2θ.

zhHant.q: 平面鏡轉動 θ（入射光線不變），反射光線方向改變____。
zhHant.exp: 鏡面轉 θ，反射光偏轉 2θ。
zhHant.hint: 鏡面轉 θ，反射光偏轉 2θ。

en.q: Snell's law is written as ____.
choices: n1 sin i = n2 sin r
n1 cos i = n2 cos r
en.exp: n1 sin i = n2 sin r relates angles and refractive indices.
en.hint: n1 sin i = n2 sin r relates angles and refractive indices.

zhHant.q: 司乃耳定律的公式是____。
choices: n1 sin i = n2 sin r
n1 cos i = n2 cos r
zhHant.exp: n1 sin i = n2 sin r 連結折射率與角度。
zhHant.hint: n1 sin i = n2 sin r 連結折射率與角度。

en.q: When light enters an optically denser medium (n2 > n1), it bends ____ the normal.
en.exp: In a denser medium the ray is closer to the normal.
en.hint: In a denser medium the ray is closer to the normal.

zhHant.q: 光進入光密介質（n2 > n1）時，向法線哪方偏折？
zhHant.exp: 進入光密介質時光線較靠近法線。
zhHant.hint: 進入光密介質時光線較靠近法線。

en.q: The refractive index n of a medium is defined as n = ____.
en.exp: n = speed of light in vacuum / speed in the medium.
en.hint: n = speed of light in vacuum / speed in the medium.

zhHant.q: 折射率 n 的定義為 n = ____。
zhHant.exp: n = 真空光速 / 介質中光速。
zhHant.hint: n = 真空光速 / 介質中光速。

en.q: When viewing an object under water from above, it appears ____ than its real depth.
en.exp: Refraction at the surface makes the image appear closer (apparent depth < real depth).
en.hint: Refraction at the surface makes the image appear closer (apparent depth < real depth).

zhHant.q: 從水上俯視水底物體，它看起來比實際深度____。
zhHant.exp: 折射使視深小於實深。
zhHant.hint: 折射使視深小於實深。

en.q: In a medium with higher refractive index, light travels ____.
choices: at the same speed as in vacuum
en.exp: Larger n means smaller speed v = c/n.
en.hint: Larger n means smaller speed v = c/n.

zhHant.q: 折射率較大的介質中，光速____。
zhHant.exp: n 越大，v = c/n 越小。
zhHant.hint: n 越大，v = c/n 越小。

en.q: If light strikes a boundary at normal incidence (i = 0°), it ____.
en.exp: At i = 0°, the refracted ray continues along the normal.
en.hint: At i = 0°, the refracted ray continues along the normal.

zhHant.q: 光垂直入射介面（i = 0°）時，____。
zhHant.exp: i = 0° 時折射光沿法線方向。
zhHant.hint: i = 0° 時折射光沿法線方向。

en.q: When light passes from a denser to a less dense medium, it bends ____ the normal.
en.exp: Leaving a denser medium, the ray moves away from the normal.
en.hint: Leaving a denser medium, the ray moves away from the normal.

zhHant.q: 光由光密進入光疏介質時，向法線____偏折。
zhHant.exp: 進入光疏介質時光線遠離法線。
zhHant.hint: 進入光疏介質時光線遠離法線。

en.q: If the direction of light along a refracted path is reversed, the ray ____.
en.exp: Refraction obeys reversibility of light rays.
en.hint: Refraction obeys reversibility of light rays.

zhHant.q: 沿折射光路反向傳播時，光線____。
zhHant.exp: 折射符合光路可逆性。
zhHant.hint: 折射符合光路可逆性。

en.q: For most calculations in air, the refractive index is taken as approximately ____.
en.exp: Air ≈ 1; glass and water have n > 1.
en.hint: Air ≈ 1; glass and water have n > 1.

zhHant.q: 在空氣中的計算通常取折射率約為____。
zhHant.exp: 空氣 n ≈ 1。
zhHant.hint: 空氣 n ≈ 1。

section: Angle increases in less dense medium

en.q: When light enters a less dense medium from a more dense one, the angle in the less dense medium is ____ the angle of incidence in the more dense medium.
en.exp: From Snell's law, sin r > sin i when n2 < n1.
en.hint: From Snell's law, sin r > sin i when n2 < n1.

zhHant.q: 光由光密進入光疏時，光疏中的折射角與光密中的入射角相比____。
zhHant.exp: n2 < n1 時 sin r > sin i。
zhHant.hint: n2 < n1 時 sin r > sin i。

en.q: Total internal reflection can occur when light travels from ____ to ____.
en.exp: TIR needs light incident in the optically denser medium.
en.hint: TIR needs light incident in the optically denser medium.

zhHant.q: 全內反射發生時，光由____進入____。
zhHant.exp: 須從光密介質射向光疏介質。
zhHant.hint: 須從光密介質射向光疏介質。

en.q: The critical angle C when light goes from medium 1 (denser) to medium 2 (rarer) satisfies sin C = ____.
en.exp: sin C = n_rare / n_dense (second medium / first).
en.hint: sin C = n_rare / n_dense (second medium / first).

zhHant.q: 光由介質 1（光密）到介質 2（光疏）的臨界角 C 滿足 sin C = ____。
zhHant.exp: sin C = n疏 / n密。
zhHant.hint: sin C = n疏 / n密。

en.q: TIR occurs when the angle of incidence in the denser medium is ____ the critical angle.
en.exp: i > C gives total internal reflection.
en.hint: i > C gives total internal reflection.

zhHant.q: 當光密中的入射角____臨界角時發生全內反射。
zhHant.exp: i > C 時全內反射。
zhHant.hint: i > C 時全內反射。

en.q: At the critical angle, the refracted ray along the boundary has an angle of refraction of ____.
en.exp: At C the refracted ray grazes the surface (r = 90°).
en.hint: At C the refracted ray grazes the surface (r = 90°).

zhHant.q: 在臨界角時，折射光線沿界面的折射角為____。
zhHant.exp: 臨界角時 r = 90°。
zhHant.hint: 臨界角時 r = 90°。

en.q: Optical fibres use repeated ____ to keep light inside the core.
en.exp: Core-cladding TIR guides light along the fibre.
en.hint: Core-cladding TIR guides light along the fibre.

zhHant.q: 光纖利用重複的____使光在芯內傳播。
zhHant.exp: 芯與包層間的全內反射導光。
zhHant.hint: 芯與包層間的全內反射導光。

en.q: Diamonds sparkle partly because their high refractive index gives a ____ critical angle.
en.exp: Small C means more TIR inside the gem.
en.hint: Small C means more TIR inside the gem.

zhHant.q: 鑽石耀眼，部分原因是高折射率使臨界角____。
zhHant.exp: 臨界角小，內部全內反射多。
zhHant.hint: 臨界角小，內部全內反射多。

en.q: When light travels from a less dense to a more dense medium, total internal reflection ____.
en.exp: TIR requires dense-to-rare incidence.
en.hint: TIR requires dense-to-rare incidence.

zhHant.q: 光由光疏進入光密時，全內反射____。
zhHant.exp: 全內反射需光密→光疏。
zhHant.hint: 全內反射需光密→光疏。

en.q: If i < C (dense to rare), both reflection and ____ occur at the boundary.
en.exp: Below C there is partial reflection and refraction.
en.hint: Below C there is partial reflection and refraction.

zhHant.q: 光密→光疏且 i < C 時，界面上同時有反射和____。
zhHant.exp: i < C 時為部分反射與折射。
zhHant.hint: i < C 時為部分反射與折射。

en.q: A periscope using plane mirrors relies on ____ at each mirror.
en.exp: Standard periscopes use reflection, not TIR.
en.hint: Standard periscopes use reflection, not TIR.

zhHant.q: 用平面鏡的潛望鏡在每面鏡上依靠____。
zhHant.exp: 一般潛望鏡用反射。
zhHant.hint: 一般潛望鏡用反射。

en.q: For light from water (n ≈ 1.33) to air (n ≈ 1), sin C is approximately ____.
en.exp: sin C = n_air / n_water ≈ 1/1.33.
en.hint: sin C = n_air / n_water ≈ 1/1.33.

zhHant.q: 光由水（n ≈ 1.33）到空氣（n ≈ 1），sin C 約為____。
zhHant.exp: sin C = n空氣 / n水。
zhHant.hint: sin C = n空氣 / n水。

en.q: A convex lens is also called a ____ lens.
en.exp: Convex lenses converge parallel rays toward a focus.
en.hint: Convex lenses converge parallel rays toward a focus.

zhHant.q: 凸透鏡又稱____透鏡。
zhHant.exp: 凸透鏡使平行光會聚。
zhHant.hint: 凸透鏡使平行光會聚。

en.q: For a convex lens, a ray parallel to the principal axis refracts through ____.
en.exp: Rule 1: parallel in, focus out.
en.hint: Rule 1: parallel in, focus out.

zhHant.q: 凸透鏡：平行於主軸的光線折射後通過____。
zhHant.exp: 規則一：平行入、焦點出。
zhHant.hint: 規則一：平行入、焦點出。

en.q: A ray through the focus F of a convex lens emerges ____ to the principal axis.
en.exp: Rule 2: focus in, parallel out.
en.hint: Rule 2: focus in, parallel out.

zhHant.q: 通過凸透鏡焦點 F 的光線折射後與主軸____。
zhHant.exp: 規則二：焦點入、平行出。
zhHant.hint: 規則二：焦點入、平行出。

en.q: A ray through the optical centre C of a thin lens ____.
en.exp: Rule 3: C in, C out — undeviated.
en.hint: Rule 3: C in, C out — undeviated.

zhHant.q: 通過薄透鏡光心 C 的光線____。
zhHant.exp: 規則三：光心入、光心出。
zhHant.hint: 規則三：光心入、光心出。

en.q: A real object beyond 2f from a convex lens forms a real image that is ____.
choices: inverted and diminished between f and 2f
en.exp: u > 2f: real, inverted, diminished image between f and 2f.
en.hint: u > 2f: real, inverted, diminished image between f and 2f.

zhHant.q: 實物在凸透鏡 2f 以外時，像為____。
choices: 倒立縮小且在 f 與 2f 之間
zhHant.exp: u > 2f：實像、倒立、縮小。
zhHant.hint: u > 2f：實像、倒立、縮小。

en.q: When u = 2f for a convex lens, the image is formed at ____.
en.exp: u = 2f gives v = 2f, same size inverted real image.
en.hint: u = 2f gives v = 2f, same size inverted real image.

zhHant.q: 凸透鏡 u = 2f 時，像成在____。
zhHant.exp: u = v = 2f，等大倒立實像。
zhHant.hint: u = v = 2f，等大倒立實像。

en.q: When f < u < 2f for a convex lens, the image is real, inverted and ____.
en.exp: f < u < 2f gives enlarged real image beyond 2f.
en.hint: f < u < 2f gives enlarged real image beyond 2f.

zhHant.q: 凸透鏡 f < u < 2f 時，像為實像、倒立且____。
zhHant.exp: f < u < 2f：放大實像。
zhHant.hint: f < u < 2f：放大實像。

en.q: When the object distance is shorter than the focal length of a convex lens (u < f), the image is ____.
en.exp: u < f: virtual magnifying-glass image.
en.hint: u < f: virtual magnifying-glass image.

zhHant.q: 物距短於凸透鏡焦距（u < f）時，像為____。
zhHant.exp: u < f：虛像、正立、放大（放大鏡）。
zhHant.hint: u < f：虛像、正立、放大（放大鏡）。

en.q: In the lens formula 1/u + 1/v = 1/f, for a convex lens when the image is real, what is the sign of 1/v?
en.exp: Real images have v > 0, so 1/v is positive.
en.hint: Real images have v > 0, so 1/v is positive.

zhHant.q: 透鏡公式 1/u + 1/v = 1/f 中，凸透鏡成實像時，1/v 的符號為____。
zhHant.exp: 實像 v > 0，故 1/v 為正。
zhHant.hint: 實像 v > 0，故 1/v 為正。

en.q: In the lens formula 1/u + 1/v = 1/f, for a convex lens when the image is virtual, what is the sign of 1/v?
en.exp: Virtual images have v < 0, so 1/v is negative.
en.hint: Virtual images have v < 0, so 1/v is negative.

zhHant.q: 透鏡公式 1/u + 1/v = 1/f 中，凸透鏡成虛像時，1/v 的符號為____。
zhHant.exp: 虛像 v < 0，故 1/v 為負。
zhHant.hint: 虛像 v < 0，故 1/v 為負。

en.q: A camera uses a convex lens to form a ____ image on the sensor.
en.exp: Objects are usually beyond 2f → real inverted image.
en.hint: Objects are usually beyond 2f → real inverted image.

zhHant.q: 相機用凸透鏡在感光元件上成____。
zhHant.exp: 物通常在 2f 外→實像倒立。
zhHant.hint: 物通常在 2f 外→實像倒立。

en.q: A concave lens is a ____ lens.
en.exp: Concave lenses spread out parallel rays.
en.hint: Concave lenses spread out parallel rays.

zhHant.q: 凹透鏡是____透鏡。
zhHant.exp: 凹透鏡使平行光發散。
zhHant.hint: 凹透鏡使平行光發散。

en.q: For a real object, a concave lens always forms a ____ image.
en.exp: Diverging lens → virtual, upright, smaller image.
en.hint: Diverging lens → virtual, upright, smaller image.

zhHant.q: 凹透鏡對實物總是成____。
zhHant.exp: 發散透鏡→虛像、正立、縮小。
zhHant.hint: 發散透鏡→虛像、正立、縮小。

en.q: In the lens formula 1/u + 1/v = 1/f, for a concave lens, what is the sign of 1/v?
en.exp: Concave lenses form virtual images with v < 0, so 1/v is negative.
en.hint: Concave lenses form virtual images with v < 0, so 1/v is negative.

zhHant.q: 透鏡公式 1/u + 1/v = 1/f 中，凹透鏡成像時，1/v 的符號為____。
zhHant.exp: 凹透鏡成虛像，v < 0，故 1/v 為負。
zhHant.hint: 凹透鏡成虛像，v < 0，故 1/v 為負。

en.q: After passing through a concave lens, a ray parallel to the axis appears to come from ____.
choices: the focal point F on the same side as the object
en.exp: Parallel in, diverges as if from F.
en.hint: Parallel in, diverges as if from F.

zhHant.q: 凹透鏡：平行主軸的光線折射後好像來自____。
zhHant.exp: 平行入、發散似從 F 出發。
zhHant.hint: 平行入、發散似從 F 出發。

en.q: Concave lenses are used in spectacles to correct ____.
en.exp: Myopia needs diverging lenses to move the focus onto the retina.
en.hint: Myopia needs diverging lenses to move the focus onto the retina.

zhHant.q: 凹透鏡眼鏡用於矯正____。
zhHant.exp: 近視需發散透鏡使焦點後移到視網膜。
zhHant.hint: 近視需發散透鏡使焦點後移到視網膜。

en.q: The virtual image from a concave lens is always on the ____ side as the object.
en.exp: Virtual image lies between F and the lens on the object side.
en.hint: Virtual image lies between F and the lens on the object side.

zhHant.q: 凹透鏡的虛像總是在物的____側。
zhHant.exp: 虛像在物與透鏡之間。
zhHant.hint: 虛像在物與透鏡之間。

en.q: A concave lens is thinner at the ____ and thicker at the edges.
en.exp: Diverging lenses are thinner in the centre.
en.hint: Diverging lenses are thinner in the centre.

zhHant.q: 凹透鏡____較薄，邊緣較厚。
zhHant.exp: 凹透鏡中間薄、邊緣厚。
zhHant.hint: 凹透鏡中間薄、邊緣厚。

en.q: Can a concave lens alone form a real image of a real object on a screen?
en.exp: Real objects always give virtual images with a diverging lens.
en.hint: Real objects always give virtual images with a diverging lens.

zhHant.q: 凹透鏡能否單獨把實物的實像成在屏幕上？
zhHant.exp: 實物經凹透鏡只能成虛像。
zhHant.hint: 實物經凹透鏡只能成虛像。

en.q: In a simple Galilean telescope, the eyepiece is often a ____ lens.
en.exp: Galilean design uses concave eyepiece with convex objective.
en.hint: Galilean design uses concave eyepiece with convex objective.

zhHant.q: 簡單伽利略望遠鏡的目鏡通常是____透鏡。
zhHant.exp: 伽利略式：凸物鏡 + 凹目鏡。
zhHant.hint: 伽利略式：凸物鏡 + 凹目鏡。

en.q: A lens with a shorter focal length has ____ diverging power.
en.exp: A shorter focal length means stronger divergence.
en.hint: A shorter focal length means stronger divergence.

zhHant.q: 焦距越負（絕對值越大）的凹透鏡，發散能力____。
zhHant.exp: f 越負，發散越強。
zhHant.hint: f 越負，發散越強。

en.q: Electromagnetic waves are transverse waves that can travel in ____.
en.exp: EM waves do not need a medium; they travel in vacuum.
en.hint: EM waves do not need a medium.

zhHant.q: 電磁波是橫波，可在____中傳播。
zhHant.exp: 電磁波不需介質，可在真空傳播。
zhHant.hint: 電磁波不需介質，可在真空傳播。

en.q: Which order shows increasing frequency?
choices: radio → microwave → IR → visible → UV → X-ray → gamma
en.exp: Standard EM spectrum from low to high frequency.
en.hint: Standard EM spectrum from low to high frequency.

zhHant.q: 下列哪個次序為頻率遞增？

en.q: For electromagnetic waves, when frequency increases, wavelength ____ (in the same medium).

en.hint: c = fλ; higher f means shorter λ.

zhHant.hint: c = fλ，f 大則 λ 小。

en.q: Excessive ultraviolet radiation can cause ____.

en.hint: RGB additive mixing: full red, green and blue → white.

en.q: All electromagnetic waves travel at speed c ≈ 3×10⁸ m s⁻¹ in ____.

A student observes the print on a piece of paper as shown. Which of the following statements about

(2) The object distance is equal to the focal length of the lens.
(3) When the lens is moved towards the print slightly, the print would be further magnified.

(2) is incorrect, if object distance is equal o focal length of the convex lens, no image will be forced. (3) is incorrect, 𝑓= 𝑢− 𝑣 , when 𝑢 decrease 𝑣also decrease the image size (2): Virtual image can be formed by a magnifying convex lens.
en.hint: (1) is correct, since the image is magnified, it is formed by convex lens.

en.q:
An object is placed in front of a convex lens. The image formed has the same size as the object. The
separation between the object and the image is 40 cm. Which of the following statements is/are
correct?
(1) The focal length of the convex lens is 20 cm
(2) If the object is move slightly towards the lens, the distance between the object and the image will
be larger
(3) If the object is move slightly away from the lens, the image will become smaller
en.exp: (1) is incorrect, the image has the same size as the object, the object is located at 2𝐹. Therefore, the focal length should be 10 𝑐𝑚. (2) is correct, 𝑓= 𝑢+ 𝑣 , when 10 = 18 + 𝑣 , 𝑣= 22.5 𝑐𝑚. When 𝑢 change to 18, the distance between the image and the object become 40.5 𝑐𝑚. (3) is correct, 𝑓= 𝑢+ 𝑣, when 𝑢 increase, 𝑣 decrease, the image will be smaller.
en.hint: (1) is incorrect, the image has the same size as the object, the object is located at 2𝐹.

en.q:
An object is placed in front of a lens and a plane mirror as shown. The image is formed at where the
object is. What is the focal length of the lens?
en.exp: When parallel light ray enters the lens, the light ray will be converged to the focus. The parallel light ray reflected from the mirror are converged to a point 30 𝑐𝑚 from the lens, thus the focal length of the lens is 30 𝑐𝑚.
en.hint: When parallel light ray enters the lens, the light ray will be converged to the focus.

en.q:
Which of the following statements about the following experiment is/are correct?
(1) The focal length of the convex lens is equal to the distance
between the convex lens and the lamp-housing.
(2) The brightness and shape of the image will not change if
the plane mirror is moved a little bit backwards.
(3) The shape of the image will not change if the upper half
of the lens is covered by a card.
en.exp: Only when the object is located at the principal focus of the convex lens, rays from the object are parallel to the principal axis after passing through the lens. These parallel rays are reflected by the plane mirror and then converge to the principal focus of the lens after passing through the lens once again. ∴ (1) is correct. The reflection of the parallel rays by the plane mirror is unaffected if the plane mirror is moved a little bit backwards. ∴ (2) is correct. When the convex lens and the plane mirror are placed at positions so that a clear image is formed on the screen, the size of the image equals to that of the object. ∴ (3) is incorrect.
en.hint: Only when the object is located at the principal focus of the convex lens, rays from the…

en.q:
A student places a magnifying glass 3 cm away from the print of a book. The linear magnification of
the image formed is 3. What is the distance between the print and its image?
en.exp: Since the image is magnified and erect, it is at the same side of the object and virtual. 𝑣= 3(3) = 9 𝑐𝑚 The distance between the print and the image = 9 −3 = 6 𝑐𝑚
en.hint: Since the image is magnified and erect, it is at the same side of the object and virtual.

en.q:
A real image is formed by a convex lens of a focal length of 20 cm. The distance between the object
and the image is 80 cm. What is the object distance?
en.exp: Since the image is real, it is located at the opposite side of the object. 20 = 𝑢+ 80−𝑢, 20 = 80−𝑢+𝑢 𝑢(80−𝑢) 80𝑢−𝑢2 = 1600 𝑢= 40
en.hint: Since the image is real, it is located at the opposite side of the object.

en.q:
A convex lens is used to form an image of an object clearly on a screen as shown. If the screen is
moved away from the lens, which of the following method may lead to the formation of a clear
image on the screen again?
(1) The object is fixed while the lens is moved towards the screen
(2) The object is moved towards the lens, keeping the lens position fixed
(3) The object is moved with the lens together, and towards the screen
en.exp: (1) is incorrect, 𝑓= 𝑢+ 𝑣, when 𝑢 increase, 𝑣 decrease, the image will never be located on the screen. (2) is correct, when 𝑢 decrease, 𝑣 increase, which may cause the image fall on the screen again. (3) is correct, since the object and image distance unchanged, moving the object and the lens to the screen may cause the image fall on the screen again.

en.q:
A girl is standing in front of a plane mirror which is hanging on the wall as shown in the figure. She is
just able to see her feet through the mirror. In which of the following situations can she still see her
feet through the mirror?
(1) The girl stands at the same position but raises the mirror.
(2) The girl walks towards the mirror.
(3) The girl walks away from the mirror.
en.exp: The image distance of the upper mirror is 𝑎. The image distance for the second mirror is 𝑎+ 𝑏, thus the distance of the image from the observer is 𝑎+ 𝑏+ 𝑐. The image distance of the picture is 2 + 0.5 = 2.5 𝑚. The distance between the boy and the image is 2.5 + 2 = 4.5 𝑚 (1) is incorrect, the lower edge of the mirror will not be able to reflect the light from the feet to her eyes. (2) and (3) are correct. Look at the following figures.
en.hint: The image distance of the upper mirror is 𝑎.

en.q:
A plane mirror is hanging on a wall and three light bulbs P, Q and R are situated near it as shown in the
figure below. Three of the light rays emitted from the bulbs are reflected by the upper edge of the mirror
as shown in the figure. Which of the bulbs can be seen by the observer through the mirror?
en.exp: Image formed by plane mirror is laterally inverted, thus the real time is 8 ∶05 For the light ray from his feet to be reflected to his eyes, the mirror should at least at a height half of the distance between his eyes and feet, which is 1.7 2 = 0.85 𝑚. And the mirror should be half of the height of the man. Which is 1.8 2 = 0.6 𝑚 Since the angle of incidence and reflection can only be increase as the light rays reflect at the lower points of the mirror, the reflected light rays can only bend more downwards to the observers but not upwards.

en.q:
Which of the following statements concerning total internal reflection of light is/are correct?
(1) The angle of reflection equals to the critical angle.
(2) Total internal reflection of light will not occur when light enters an optically less dense medium.
(3) The critical angle depends on the refractive index.
en.exp: C and D are incorrect. The angle of incidence from A to P should be the same as the angle of refraction from P to A. Since they come from the same formula of 𝑛𝑃sin 𝜃𝑃= 𝑛𝐴sin 𝜃𝐴. A is incorrect since light travel slower in A than in P, the light ray should bend away from normal when it enters P from A. (1) is incorrect, the angle of refraction should be same as the angle of incidence. (2) is incorrect, total internal reflection will only occur when light ray enters a less dense medium from a denser medium. (3) is correct, sin 𝐶= 𝑛.
en.hint: C and D are incorrect.

zhHant.q:
Which of the following statements concerning total internal reflection of light is/are correct?
(1) The angle of reflection equals to the critical angle.
(2) Total internal reflection of light will not occur when light enters an optically less dense medium.
(3) The critical angle depends on the refractive index.
zhHant.exp: C and D are incorrect. The angle of incidence from A to P should be the same as the angle of refraction from P to A. Since they come from the same formula of 𝑛𝑃sin 𝜃𝑃= 𝑛𝐴sin 𝜃𝐴. A is incorrect since light travel slower in A than in P, the light ray should bend away from normal when it enters P from A. (1) is incorrect, the angle of refraction should be same as the angle of incidence. (2) is incorrect, total internal reflection will only occur when light ray enters a less dense medium from a denser medium. (3) is correct, sin 𝐶= 𝑛.
zhHant.hint: C and D are incorrect.

en.q:
Which of the following statement(s) about the virtual image is/are correct?
(1) Virtual image must be erect.
(2) Virtual image cannot be captured by a camera.
(3) Virtual image can be seen by the naked eyes.
en.exp: (1): Correct. Min length of mirror = half of the height = 0.5(1.8) = 0.9 m (2): Incorrect. The image is the same size as object, regardless of object distance (3): Incorrect. He can still see his whole image, regardless of object distance For the portion above the hole, vertical distance that he can see = 2(10) = 20 cm. Therefore, the maximum interval above the hole that he can see is 5 cm For the portion below the hole, vertical distance that he can see = 2(5) = 10 cm. Therefore, maximum interval above the hole that he can see is 35 cm Required portion = from 5 cm to 35 cm (1): Correct. Virtual image formed by mirror and lens are erect (2): Incorrect. Only real image can be captured by a camera (3): Correct. We can observe the image formed by mirror through our naked eyes
en.hint: (1): Correct.

Only real image can be captured by a camera (3): Correct. We can observe the image formed by mirror through our naked eyes

en.q:
An object is placed in front of a lens and a plane mirror as shown. The image is formed at where the

en.exp: When parallel light ray enters the lens, the light ray will be converged to the focus. The parallel light ray reflected from the mirror are converged to a point 30 𝑐𝑚 from the lens, thus the focal length of the lens is 30 𝑐𝑚.
en.hint: When parallel light ray enters the lens, the light ray will be converged to the focus.

en.q:
A student places a magnifying glass 3 cm away from the print of a book. The linear magnification of
the image formed is 3. What is the distance between the print and its image?
en.exp: Since the image is magnified and erect, it is at the same side of the object and virtual. 𝑣= 3(3) = 9 𝑐𝑚 The distance between the print and the image = 9 −3 = 6 𝑐𝑚 From the graph, when 𝑢= 20, 𝑣= 20 𝑓= 20 + 𝑓= 10 𝑐𝑚
en.hint: Since the image is magnified and erect, it is at the same side of the object and virtual.

en.q:
A convex lens is used to form an image of an object clearly on a screen as shown. If the screen is
moved away from the lens, which of the following method may lead to the formation of a clear
image on the screen again?
(1) The object is fixed while the lens is moved towards the screen
(2) The object is moved towards the lens, keeping the lens position fixed
(3) The object is moved with the lens together, and towards the screen
en.exp: (1) is incorrect, 𝑓= 𝑢+ 𝑣, when 𝑢 increase, 𝑣 decrease, the image will never be located on the screen. (2) is correct, when 𝑢 decrease, 𝑣 increase, which may cause the image fall on the screen again. (3) is correct, since the object and image distance unchanged, moving the object and the lens to the screen may cause the image fall on the screen again.
en.hint: (1) is incorrect, 𝑓= 𝑢+ 𝑣, when 𝑢 increase, 𝑣 decrease, the image will never be located o…

. Which of the following

en.q:
Energy is transferred at equal rate to two substances X and Y of the same mass. The graph below shows
their temperature variations as they change from solids to liquids.
Which of the following statements are correct?
(1) The melting point of substance X is higher than that of substance Y.
(2) The specific heat capacity of solid X is smaller than that of solid Y.
(3) The specific latent heat of fusion of substance X is smaller than that of substance Y.
en.exp: (1) is incorrect, 𝑋 melt at a lower temperature in the graph. (2) is correct, the temperature of solid X increases at a higher rate than solid Y. (3) is correct, X require lesser time to completely melt.
en.hint: (1) is incorrect, 𝑋 melt at a lower temperature in the graph.

en.q:
Liquid X and Y of the same mass and temperature are put into a refrigerator to cool down. The graph
below shows the variation of the temperatures of the liquids with time. Which of the following statements

(2) The specific heat capacity of liquid Y is lower than that of liquid X.
(3) The specific latent heat of fusion of X is higher than that of Y.
en.exp: (1) is correct, X change state at a higher temperature than Y. (2) is correct, temperature of liquid Y decrease at a higher rate than liquid X. (3) is incorrect, it takes lesser time for X to change from liquid to solid completely.
en.hint: (1) is correct, X change state at a higher temperature than Y.

en.hint: (1) is correct, the energy was absorbed as latent heat of fusion.

en.q:
The specific heat capacity of aluminium is measured by the set-up as shown in the diagram below.
Which of the following cannot reduce experimental error?
choices: Add some oil to the hole for placing the thermometer and heater.
Place a polystyrene tile under the block.
Read the final temperature a few minutes after switching off the heater.
Wrap the block with cotton wool.
en.exp: (1): Total internal energy (P.E.+K.E.) is used to describe the total energy in a body. C: Measuring the temperature a few minutes after heating, which is too long in time, will increase the measurement error as heat loses to the surrounding.
en.hint: (1): Total internal energy (P.E.+K.E.) is used to describe the total energy in a body.

en.q:
To prepare cup noodles, 400 g hot water at 95 °C is poured into the paper cup that contains 120 g
noodles. Assume the room temperature is 25 °C. Ignore the heat loss to the surroundings. Estimate
the final temperature of the mixture.

  specific heat capacity of water = 4200 J kg−1 °C−1
en.exp: 𝐻𝑒𝑎𝑡 𝑙𝑜𝑠𝑠= 𝐻𝑒𝑎𝑡 𝑔𝑎𝑖𝑛 ሺ0.4ሻሺ4200ሻሺ95 −𝑇ሻ= ሺ0.12ሻሺ1800ሻሺ𝑇−25ሻ 𝑇= 87.0 ℃
en.hint: 𝐻𝑒𝑎𝑡 𝑙𝑜𝑠𝑠= 𝐻𝑒𝑎𝑡 𝑔𝑎𝑖𝑛 ሺ0.4ሻሺ4200ሻሺ95 −𝑇ሻ= ሺ0.12ሻሺ1800ሻሺ𝑇−25ሻ 𝑇= 87.0 ℃

en.q:
Some water of 2 kg at 20 °C is poured into a beaker of melting ice. The mixture has a temperature of 0 °C. Which of the
following statements is/are correct? The specific heat capacity of water is 4200 J kg−1 °C−1 and the specific latent heat of
fusion of ice is 3.34 × 105 J kg−1. Neglect any energy gained from or lost to the surroundings.
(1)
The mass of melting ice must be larger than 2 kg.
(2)
For the melting ice, the average kinetic energy due to random motion of the molecules remains unchanged.
(3)
The total internal energy of the ice and the water remains unchanged.
en.exp: (1): Energy required to cool down the water to 0°C= 2420020 = 168000J Let m kg be the minimum mass of ice melt. 𝑚ሺ3.34105ሻ= 168000 𝑚= 0.503𝑘𝑔 Therefore, the mass of ice melt can be less than 2 kg. (2): The average K.E. of the ice molecules will not change as the temperature of the mixture stay at 0°C. (3): The total internal energy is conserved as there is no heat gain or loss to the surroundings as stated.
en.hint: (1): Energy required to cool down the water to 0°C= 2420020 = 168000J Let m kg be the m…

en.q:
In an insulated container, 50 g of steam at 100 C is injected uniformly on a glass plate. What is the final temperature
of the glass plate? The mass of glass plate is 1.5 kg and its initial temperature is 25 C.
Given:   Specific latent heat of vaporization of water = 2.26  106 J kg−1
        Specific heat capacity of water = 4200 J kg−1 C−1
Specific heat capacity of glass = 840 J kg−1 C−1
en.exp: Energy released to change 100 ℃ Steam to 100 ℃ water: ΔE1 = m1 lv = 0.05 × ( 2.26 × 106 ) = 1.13 × 105 J Energy required to change 25 ℃ water to 100 ℃ water: ΔE2 = m2 cΔT = 1.5 × 840 × (100 − 25 ) = 94 500 J Since ΔE2 < ΔE1, the final mixture is 100 ℃, i.e. not all steam condensed.
en.hint: Energy released to change 100 ℃ Steam to 100 ℃ water: ΔE1 = m1 lv = 0.05 × ( 2.26 × 106 )…

en.q:
A student mixes 0.39 kg of water and 0.01 kg of ice, both at 0 °C, in a container. Then, she transfers a metal
sphere of 0.1 kg at 89.3 °C into the container and stirs the mixture. If the mixture reaches equilibrium at 2 °C,
what is the energy released by the metal sphere? Neglect the heat loss to the surroundings.
Given:
specific heat capacity of water = 4200 J kg−1 °C−1
specific heat capacity of copper = 385 J kg−1 °C−1
specific latent heat of ice = 334 000 J kg−1
en.exp: 𝐸𝑛𝑒𝑟𝑔𝑦 𝑟𝑒𝑙𝑒𝑎𝑠𝑒𝑑= 𝐸𝑛𝑒𝑟𝑔𝑦 𝑎𝑏𝑠𝑜𝑟𝑏𝑒𝑑 𝐸= ሺ0.39 + 0.01ሻሺ2ሻሺ4200ሻ+ ሺ0.01ሻሺ334000ሻ 𝐸= 6700 𝐽
en.hint: 𝐸𝑛𝑒𝑟𝑔𝑦 𝑟𝑒𝑙𝑒𝑎𝑠𝑒𝑑= 𝐸𝑛𝑒𝑟𝑔𝑦 𝑎𝑏𝑠𝑜𝑟𝑏𝑒𝑑 𝐸= ሺ0.39 + 0.01ሻሺ2ሻሺ4200ሻ+ ሺ0.01ሻሺ334000ሻ 𝐸= 6700 𝐽

en.q:
Metal block A is in contact with another metal block B, and they finally reach thermal equilibrium.
Block A experiences a temperature rise of 10 °C and block B experiences a temperature drop of 5 °C.
Assume there is no heat loss to the surroundings. Which of the following is correct?
choices: The initial temperature of A is higher than that of B.
The energy gained by A is larger than the energy lost by B.
The average kinetic energy of A increases during the heat exchange process.
The internal energy of B remains the same during the heat exchange process
en.exp: A: As metal block A experiences a temperature rise, it is gaining energy. It means that the initial temperature of A is lower than that of B B: By the conservation of energy, energy loss = energy gain C: The higher the temperature, the higher the average KE is. D: PE of B remains unchanged. As temperature decreases, the average KE decreases, thus internal energy decreases
en.hint: A: As metal block A experiences a temperature rise, it is gaining energy.

zhHant.exp: A: As metal block A experiences a temperature rise, it is gaining energy. D: PE of B remains unchanged. As temperature decreases, the average KE decreases, thus internal energy decreases
zhHant.hint: A: As metal block A experiences a temperature rise, it is gaining energy.

en.q:
A liquid X at 90 °C is mixed with a liquid Y at 30 °C. The final mixture has a temperature of 70 °C.
Neglecting any heat exchange with the surroundings, which of the following statements is correct?
choices: At the final temperature, X and Y has the same internal energy.
The heat lost by X is less than the heat gained by Y.
X has a higher specific heat capacity than Y.
X has a higher heat capacity than Y.
en.exp: Without heat loss, by the conservation of energy Heat gain by liquid Y = Heat loss by liquid X CX (90 − 70) = CY (70 − 30), where CX and CY are heat capacity of liquid X and Y CX = 2CY. Since mass is unknown, value of specific heat capacity of liquid X and Y cannot be determined. Therefore, B and C are incorrect Internal energy is affected by mass, KE and PE. As the mass is unknown, value of internal energy is unknown
en.hint: Without heat loss, by the conservation of energy Heat gain by liquid Y = Heat loss by liq…

en.q:
Substances X and Y of the same mass are heated at the same rate. The graphs below show their temperature–
time graphs. Initially, they are in solid state. Which of the following physical quantities of substance X are
higher than that of substance Y according to the graph?
choices: The specific latent heat of fusion and the specific heat capacity in solid state
The specific latent heat of fusion and the specific heat capacity in liquid state
The melting point and the specific heat capacity in solid state
The melting point and the specific heat capacity in liquid state
en.exp: The length of horizontal line in Y is longer than that in X. As both have the same mass and are heated at the same rate, specific latent heat of Y is greater than that of X. A and B are incorrect The height of horizontal line represents melting point. Therefore, X has a higher melting point 𝑠𝑝𝑒𝑐𝑖𝑓𝑖𝑐 ℎ𝑒𝑎𝑡 𝑐𝑎𝑝𝑎𝑐𝑖𝑡𝑦 ∝ Slope at solid/liquid state Slope of solid X > Slope of solid Y 𝑐𝑋ሺ𝑠ሻ< 𝑐𝑌ሺ𝑠ሻ Slope of liquid X < Slope of liquid Y 𝑐𝑋ሺ𝑙ሻ> 𝑐𝑌ሺ𝑙ሻ D is correct (1): The y-value of the first horizontal line of X is greater than that of the first horizontal line of Y. As there is not any temperature change during fusion, it indicates that X has a higher melting point (2): The slope of Y in liquid state is greater than that of X in liquid state. By Pt = C𝛥𝑇, 𝑆𝑙𝑜𝑝𝑒= 𝛥𝑇 𝑡= 𝑃 𝐶. As P to X and Y is the same, Y should have a smaller heat capacity than X (3): As the masses of X and Y are unknown, the specific latent heat of fusion cannot be determined
en.hint: The length of horizontal line in Y is longer than that in X.

zhHant.q:
Substances X and Y of the same mass are heated at the same rate. The graphs below show their temperature–
time graphs. Initially, they are in solid state. Which of the following physical quantities of substance X are
higher than that of substance Y according to the graph?
choices: The specific latent heat of fusion and the specific heat capacity in solid state
The specific latent heat of fusion and the specific heat capacity in liquid state
The melting point and the specific heat capacity in solid state
The melting point and the specific heat capacity in liquid state
zhHant.exp: The length of horizontal line in Y is longer than that in X. As both have the same mass and are heated at the same rate, specific latent heat of Y is greater than that of X. A and B are incorrect The height of horizontal line represents melting point. Therefore, X has a higher melting point 𝑠𝑝𝑒𝑐𝑖𝑓𝑖𝑐 ℎ𝑒𝑎𝑡 𝑐𝑎𝑝𝑎𝑐𝑖𝑡𝑦 ∝ Slope at solid/liquid state Slope of solid X > Slope of solid Y 𝑐𝑋ሺ𝑠ሻ< 𝑐𝑌ሺ𝑠ሻ Slope of liquid X < Slope of liquid Y 𝑐𝑋ሺ𝑙ሻ> 𝑐𝑌ሺ𝑙ሻ D is correct (1): The y-value of the first horizontal line of X is greater than that of the first horizontal line of Y. As there is not any temperature change during fusion, it indicates that X has a higher melting point (2): The slope of Y in liquid state is greater than that of X in liquid state. By Pt = C𝛥𝑇, 𝑆𝑙𝑜𝑝𝑒= 𝛥𝑇 𝑡= 𝑃 𝐶. As P to X and Y is the same, Y should have a smaller heat capacity than X (3): As the masses of X and Y are unknown, the specific latent heat of fusion cannot be determined
zhHant.hint: The length of horizontal line in Y is longer than that in X.

en.q:
Two substances P and Q, both in solid form initially, are heated up by two heaters of equal power. The figure
shows how their temperatures vary with time.
Which of the following statements about P and Q must be correct?
(1) P has a lower melting point than Q.
(2) P has a larger specific latent heat of fusion than Q.
(3) The solid form of P has a smaller heat capacity than that of Q.
en.exp: The stable temperature means the substance is changing its state. While P has a lower stable temperature than Q, so P has a lower melting point than Q. Thus, (1) is correct. Masses of P and Q aren’t given, so specific latent heat of fusion can’t be determined. Thus, (2) is incorrect ∆𝑇 𝑡= 𝑃 𝐶 , 𝑠𝑙𝑜𝑝𝑒= 𝑃 𝐶
en.hint: The stable temperature means the substance is changing its state.

en.q:
A glass rod is heated at one side. Some time later, the other side of the rod becomes hot. Explain this
phenomenon in terms of particle motion.
(3 marks)
en.answer: When one side of the rod is heated, atoms at that side vibrate faster.
en.exp:
A glass rod is heated at one side. Some time later, the other side of the rod becomes hot. Explain this
phenomenon in terms of particle motion.
(3 marks)
When one side of the rod is heated, atoms at that side vibrate faster.
They hit the slower neighbouring atoms and make them vibrate more rapidly.
This process continues and atoms at the other end of the rod are eventually set
to vibrate quickly.
Therefore, the temperature at the other end of the rod increases.
en.hint: A glass rod is heated at one side.

zhHant.q:
A glass rod is heated at one side. Some time later, the other side of the rod becomes hot. Explain this
phenomenon in terms of particle motion.
(3 marks)

zhHant.exp:

(3 marks)

A student carries out an experiment to determine the specific heat capacity of copper. He heats the copper
block to 100 °C with boiling water (Figure a). Then, he places the block in a cup of water and measures the
temperature rise of the water (Figure b).
The following results are obtained:
mass of the copper block = 80 g
mass of water inside the polystyrene cup = 150 g
initial water temperature = 18 °C
final water temperature = 21.5 °C
Find the specific heat capacity of copper assuming that the polystyrene cup has a negligible mass.
Compare the experimental value with the standard value and account for any differences between them.
(6 marks)
(The specific heat capacity of water is 4200 J kg−1 °C−1. The standard value of the specific heat capacity
of copper is 390 J kg−1 °C−1.)
